headlines.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses GOP primary in Louisiana, as two rivals advance to a runoff
headlines.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict President Donald Trump on impeachment charges in 2021, has lost his Republican primary in Louisiana, as two challengers aligned with Trump advanced to a runoff.Rep.

headlines.title: Senate parliamentarian nixes Trump’s ballroom fund in budget bill
headlines.description: A GOP bill seeking $1 billion for the Secret Service to help finance President Donald Trump’s White House ballroom is in jeopardy as it faces pushback from a top Senate official.

headlines.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy spent 20 years in public office. One decision led to his demise.
headlines.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s primary loss Saturday brings an end to a two-decade career in public office that was ultimately defined by tensions with President Donald Trump

headlines.title: Election denier Tina Peters set for release after Colorado governor commutes sentence
headlines.description: Democratic Gov. Jared Polis had previously called Peters’ 9-year prison sentence “harsh.” President Trump has repeatedly demanded she be released.

headlines.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy speaks after Louisiana primary loss
headlines.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy spoke out after losing the Louisiana primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a runoff. Cassidy spoke about democracy during his concession speak, stressing that he was not “claiming the election was stolen” after a loss.

headlines.title: NBC News projects Louisiana GOP Senate race advances to runoff, Cassidy out
headlines.description: Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy loses his primary as Rep. Julia Letlow and state Treasurer John Fleming advance to a June 27 runoff. NBC News' Steve Kornacki breaks down the race data.

headlines.title: Supreme Court Rejects Virginia Democrats' New Voting Map Bid
headlines.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Friday that a new congressional map approved by Virginia voters cannot be used. The high court found legal flaws in the process leading up to the referendum. The new map was intended to maximize Democratic-leaning districts.

headlines.title: The war is on hold, but Iran’s internal crackdown is only deepening
headlines.description: While the Iranian regime has been battling its external enemies, Israel and the U.S., it has also been ramping up a deadly crackdown on those seen as the enemy within.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Trump says ISIS second-in-command killed in Africa
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said late Friday that a top Islamic State group commander has been killed in Africa in a joint operation with Nigeria’s armed forces

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy faces a primary for the first time since his Trump impeachment vote
headlines.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s political career is at stake on Saturday as Louisiana voters weigh in on the Republican for the first time since he voted to convict President Donald Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial
